name: Arnold Press
resistance_profile: Rotating dumbbell press adding range through the delt.
execution_steps: Sit or stand tall holding dumbbells at chin height, palms facing you and elbows in front of your body.
Brace your core and keep your ribs down.
Press the dumbbells up while rotating your palms to face forward.
Finish with arms extended overhead, palms forward, without harshly locking the elbows.
Reverse the rotation on the way down, returning to palms-in at the bottom under control.
good_when: You want extra front-delt range and time under tension from the rotation.
You want variety within a dumbbell overhead-press slot.
Your shoulders tolerate rotation and you can control moderate loads well.
bad_when: You are chasing maximal overhead load or strength (a straight barbell or dumbbell press is better).
You have shoulder impingement aggravated by rotating under load.
You are a novice still learning the basic overhead press pattern.
